warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices (most electrophilic site f⁺) for pyridine were computed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier "standard"; density-fitting on) using the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.  

Method provenance:
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor, Mulliken partition.  
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit)  
Geometry provenance: single-point on the supplied pyridine.xyz coordinates (no re-optimization).  
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (cation and anion states: +1/2 and −1/2, respectively, for f⁺/f⁻ evaluation).  
Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit backend.  
Convergence: all three single points (neutral, cation, anion) reported SCF convergence; integrity checks report overall status "ok" (trustworthy = true).  
Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim above.  

Headline result (most electrophilic atom):  
Atom 1 (N) f⁺ = 0.48422572 (dimensionless).  

Reproducibility: the job was launched with the command stored in the result JSON (see cli_invocation); the live engine output was streamed to the .out file indicated during execution.  

No literature comparison requested or provided.
